extraction.one_line_summary: CHERNY Josef won from 14-rider breakaway after solo attack with 22.5km remaining, gaining 41 seconds maximum advantage over chasers.
tactical_patterns: BORA abandoned SAGAN's maglia ciclomino chase with 50km remaining, signaling sprint jersey prioritization thresholds in Grand Tour finals.
CHERNY attacked established 14-rider break at 22.5km rather than traditional final 10km, exploiting flat stage fatigue dynamics.
GC teams entirely disengaged on flat Stage 19 despite TAO 119 seconds back, revealing break distance required for defensive activation.

rider_signals.CHERNY Josef: positive: First GT win at age 27 for CCC's first stage win in two seasons suggests equipment/support issues previously limited results.
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: BORA abandoned maglia ciclomino chase 50km out on his last realistic opportunity, indicating team assessed points gap insurmountable.
rider_signals.KELDERMAN Wilco: neutral: 12-second maglia rosa lead with 119-second cushion to 4th suggests two-rider defense strategy viable for final mountains.
rider_signals.HINDLEY Jai: neutral: SUNWEB running dual GC cards 12 seconds apart creates complex final stage tactics against external threats.
rider_signals.GEOGHEGAN HART Tao: negative: 119 seconds down with two mountain stages remaining reveals INEOS lacking traditional mountain dominance to close gaps.
